mnemonics: EVERY Class Has OBJECT DNA - The Object class is the ultimate superclass from which EVERY other class in Java inherits, providing its fundamental DNA like equals(), hashCode(), and toString().
keyPoints: The Object class, found in the java.lang package, is the root of the Java class hierarchy.
All Java classes, whether built-in or user-defined, are direct or indirect subclasses of Object.
It provides critical default implementations for methods like equals(), hashCode(), toString(), and getClass(), which can be overridden.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to override both equals() and hashCode() together, leading to inconsistent behavior when objects are used in hash-based collections.
Assuming the default equals() method (which uses ==) compares object content rather than object reference.
